page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c2g1.large is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.gn5i family. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 2 dedicated vCPUs. The system memory configuration consists of 8.0 GB of RAM, establishing a ratio of 4.0 GB of memory per core.

For hardware acceleration, the instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA P4 GPU containing 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. It does not feature any local storage, meaning all operating system and application data must reside on network-attached block storage. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.gn5i-c2g1.large serves as an entry-level option for GPU-accelerated workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation guarantees consistent processing performance without resource sharing. However, the lack of local storage and the modest 1 Gbps network bandwidth represent significant tradeoffs for data-intensive applications. The 8 GB of system memory and 8 GB of VRAM also limit the instance to smaller models and lighter parallel processing tasks.

This instance configuration is suited for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration. Appropriate use cases include small-scale deep learning inference, video transcoding, entry-level graphics rendering, and lightweight CUDA-based parallel computing tasks that do not demand high network throughput or local storage performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c2g1.large is a GPU-accelerated instance within the ecs.gn5i family, running on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration features 2 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA P4 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. It does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. This instance offers entry-level GPU capabilities with dedicated CPU resources, presenting a cost-efficient option for workloads that require hardware acceleration without massive memory or storage demands. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage and modest network speeds. It is suitable for small-scale deep learning inference, video transcoding, and lightweight parallel computing workloads.
